text.title: csharp-xunit-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-xunit-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Over-specifying Verify calls on implementation-detail interactions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Moq.Verify on internal calls couples tests to implementation; refactoring breaks tests even when behavior is correct.

failure-mode.description: Stubbing extension methods or non-virtual members with Moq.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Moq generates "Expression tree may not contain..." errors. Wrap in an interface instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces useless failure output. Use Assert.Equal(expected, actual) or FluentAssertions .Should().Be().

failure-mode.description: Generating _mock.Setup(...) for every dependency method including ones the SUT never calls.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Over-specified mocks make tests break when refactoring adds or removes method calls that are not part of the public contract.

failure-mode.description: Generating duplicate [InlineData] rows with the same logical case under different labels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicates inflate test count without adding coverage signal; they hide the actual set of cases being exercised.

failure-mode.description: Using WebApplicationFactory without public partial class Program {} in .NET 6+ minimal hosting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The test assembly cannot see Program unless it is public or has InternalsVisibleTo set; integration tests fail to compile silently.
